heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 18 July 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The ongoing dry stretch, 19 days and counting started on 03 July 2026 and ranks 2nd in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 3 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C), set on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwave right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 28 June 2026, with only 68 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (206°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 8.0°C) and outside temperature (down 6.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 22%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 26421 lux) and cloud levels (up 2790 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 24046 lux) and cloud levels (up 1962 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2790m lower than yesterday, 1962.2m lower than the past week, 1300.5m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1974m to a maximum of 8460m, averaging 2400m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4360m and 3700m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Expect mostly clear skies with high cloud.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, air moisture should feel ok and little or no wind. Possible light showers, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and some sunny spells will occur. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
